carbon_pricing.scheme_name: Nilai Ekonomi Karbon (NEK) Trading Scheme - Intensity-based ETS
carbon_pricing.power_sector_coverage_notes: Phase 1 (2023-2024): Coal plants ≥25 MW connected to PLN grid only. Gas and oil plants NOT covered until Phase 2 (2025-2027) and Phase 3 (2028-2030) respectively. Both PLN-owned and IPP plants included; captive (off-grid) exempt until Phase 2.
carbon_pricing.notes: Intensity-based ETS launched Feb 2023 covers 146 coal installations from 63 entities (~67.6% coal capacity, ~37% national generation). IDX Carbon exchange trading started Sept 2023. Allowance caps set too lenient - most plants receive sufficient free allocations, creating weak price signals. Carbon tax (IDR 30,000/tCO2e) enacted in Presidential Regulation 98/2021 but NOT implemented. Gas plants coverage begins Phase 2 (2025-2027). Effective price ~IDR 60,000/tCO2e (~$4/tCO2e) but limited trading activity.
carbon_pricing.source: ICAP; IEA; IEEFA (September 2025); IDX Carbon; MEMR Regulation 16/2022; verified January 2026

coal.notes: New coal at $50-64/MWh with DMO; captive coal $77/MWh without DMO access

coal_existing_variable.notes: DMO subsidized: $22-25/MWh; at international prices: $60-90/MWh; PLN 2023 avg $48/MWh

lcoe_benchmarks.notes: Solar+storage at $54-59 competitive with new coal $57-64 per Berkeley/GSPP study. PLN solar auctions declined 78% from $250 (2015) to $56 (2022). Floating solar under $40/MWh. Indonesia cost premiums 50-100% above regional peers due to LCR, financing costs, import duties.
lcoe_benchmarks.source: IRENA Renewable Power Generation Costs 2024; BNEF; IESR; PLN RUPTL; Berkeley GSPP study

national_targets.emissions_cap_2030_note: 250 MtCO2 for on-grid only; excludes ~30 GW captive coal
national_targets.notes: 20+ ppt gap between JETP (44% RE by 2030) and NDC (19-23%). Peak emissions slipped from 2030 (JETP) to 2037 (official). Net-zero 2050 power sector conditional on international finance. JETP CIPP explicitly non-binding.
national_targets.source: JETP CIPP 2023; Indonesia 2nd NDC (Oct 2025); Climate Action Tracker

jetp_commitments.notes: Only 1.15% of $20B committed has been disbursed as grants. 1.7 GW early coal retirement funded (down from 5 GW proposal). No direct disbursement to Indonesian government. Indonesia Special Envoy called JETP a 'failed program'.
jetp_commitments.source: JETP CIPP; IEEFA; Climate Action Tracker

grid_context.electricity_market_structure: State monopoly single-buyer; PLN controls 98% of distribution; IPPs sell to PLN under take-or-pay PPAs
grid_context.notes: PLN coal share (44.9%) vs national (53%) gap due to captive coal tripling from 5.5 GW (2019) to 16.6 GW (2024). 20+ GW more captive planned. Grid EF 0.682 tCO2/MWh nearly double G20 average.
grid_context.source: Ember Global Electricity Data 2024; PLN Statistics 2024; MEMR; GEM

data_quality.notes: LCOE for wind less reliable due to only 154 MW installed. Coal variable costs depend heavily on DMO policy. JETP targets significantly exceed official NDC commitments.
